prose:
**Furan frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO gap from a DFT frontier-orbitals calculation. Level of theory: tier `standard`, **b3lyp/def2-tzvp**. Geometry provenance: fixed user-supplied XYZ file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz` (9 atoms); this was a frontier-orbitals calculation at that input geometry, not a geometry optimization. Environment: **gas phase** (`solvent`: null in the tool result). Charge and multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1. Software/backend: PySCF (`program`: `pyscf`; version not returned by the tool). SCF convergence: converged = true, SCF cycles = 9. Engine integrity verdict: `trustworthy: true`.

Headline result: **HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV**.

Orbital energies returned by chemkit: HOMO = -6.3597638 eV; LUMO = -0.1024036 eV. The tool also returned electronic energy = -6261.7289 eV = -230.11429 Hartree.

Warnings: no warnings were returned by the tool result.

Reproducibility: chemkit CLI invocation returned by the tool was `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --solvent gas /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpsj31eadw/live.json`. The chemkit tool response did not include a live `.out` log path, so I cannot truthfully report one.
